mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved earnings expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 351461.0M as of Sept 2005 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) crossing above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% as of Sept 2005, near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with rising unemployment pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 3.5% combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.62% and CPIAUCSL at 198.5 as of Sept 2005
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL momentum) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerating below 0.1% month-over-month signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential earnings compression across cyclical sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns occur in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerating simultaneously signal broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors, driving positive equity returns through earnings growth and reduced recession probability over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2009.0K units and RSAFS at 351461.0M as of Aug-Sept 2005
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in < 55% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS show month-over-month growth, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating to below 50K new jobs per month signals labor market weakness and reduced consumer income growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: Negative 5d returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation trend) remaining above 2.0% signals restrictive monetary conditions that suppress equity valuations through elevated cost of capital and reduced corporate investment, producing negative 60-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.62% with CPIAUCSL at 198.5 implies real rate near 2.0%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy regime with elevated real rates
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns occur in > 60% of instances with real FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, or the real rate interaction shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized signals weakening economic momentum and earnings growth, triggering equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession probability increases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns occur in > 65% of instances with GDPC1 growth below 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
